summary: Dependency-confusion proof-of-concept claiming an unclaimed internal name
details: Package opt-archetype-check@9.9.0 is a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept that occupies an npm name referenced as an internal dependency by another organization. The tarball contains only package.json and README.md; there is no main entry, no scripts (preinstall/install/postinstall/prepare), and no source code, so installing or requiring the current version performs no install-time or load-time action. The README explicitly states the package was published to claim an unclaimed name referenced by an official internal repository and describes an intended postinstall that would collect system information — but no such script is declared in this version. The risk is name-squat amplification: if a build pipeline misresolves the internal name to this public package, future versions could add a payload via a published update. Current version has no installer-side payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
